records.ety: [F. certifier, LL. certificare; L. certus certain + facere to make. See Certain, and cf. Certificate, v. t.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Certified (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Certifying.]

defs: To give certain information to; to assure; to make certain.
quotes: We certify the king, that . . . thou shalt have no portion on this side the river.

defs: To give certain information of; to make certain, as a fact; to verify.
quotes: The industry of science at once certifies and greatly extends our knowledge of the vastness of the creation.

defs: To testify to in writing; to make a declaration concerning, in writing, under hand, or hand and seal.
quotes: The judges shall certify their opinion to the chancellor, and upon such certificate the decree is usually founded.
